instruction: Exit the bathroom, wait at the white rug in the hallway.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Exit the bathroom, wait at the white rug in the hallway. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings to understand where I am.
I'm in a bathroom with a vanity/sink on the right and what appears to be a doorway/opening on the left leading to another area. Let me look around to find the exit.

The ahead view shows the most open path with a doorway/opening on the left side (the dark opening in the mirror area leads out). The exit appears to be ahead where there's an opening leading to another room/hallway. Let me move forward toward that opening on the left side of my ahead view.
Now I can see a doorway/opening on the left leading to a hallway area (there's a room visible through the opening). The exit is to my left. Let me turn left to face the doorway.

Now I can see a passage ahead leading to a doorway/opening. This looks like the bathroom's shower/passage area leading out. There's an opening ahead that leads to another room. Let me move forward toward the doorway.

I can see the doorway ahead-right leading to a hallway/room with darker flooring. There's a shower on the left. The exit doorway is ahead and slightly to the right. Let me turn slightly right and move toward the doorway.
Now I can clearly see the doorway ahead leading out to a hallway with dark wood flooring. This is the bathroom exit. Let me move forward through the doorway.

I'm now in the doorway threshold. I can see a hallway ahead with dark wood flooring and there's a light-colored rug/area on the floor ahead. I need to continue forward into the hallway and look for the white rug. Let me move forward.

I've exited the bathroom into the hallway. I can now see a white rug on the floor ahead-right in the hallway. This is exactly the endpoint of the instruction — the white rug in the hallway. Let me move toward it a bit to be within range.
The white rug is clearly visible on the floor to the right/ahead. I'm very close to it now. Let me move a bit closer to position myself right at the white rug.
The white rug is now right in front of me, at the bottom of the view. I am at the white rug in the hallway. This is the endpoint of the instruction. Let me issue STOP.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 81 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 19, "tool_calls_remaining": 81}

text: Let me verify my placement. The instruction says "Exit the bathroom, wait at the white rug in the hallway." I've exited the bathroom and I'm at the white rug. Let me look around to confirm the white rug is right at my position.

I can confirm my situation:
- Behind me (180°) is the bathroom I exited from.
- The white rug is on the floor right in front of me (ahead view, bottom of image).
- I'm in the hallway area.

The white rug is directly at my feet in the ahead view. I've successfully exited the bathroom and I'm at the white rug in the hallway. This is the endpoint. Let me confirm STOP.
